title: USS Ford CSG Begins Atlantic Transit Toward Middle East
detail: The USS Gerald R. Ford carrier strike group, returning from operations in the Caribbean related to the Venezuela intervention, began its transatlantic crossing toward the Strait of Gibraltar and the Mediterranean. The deployment extension created an unprecedented two-carrier posture aimed at Iran, with the Ford set to complement the Lincoln already in the Arabian Sea. The Navy described it as a 'routine deployment extension' though it would push Ford past 240 days deployed.

title: Rouhani Warns of Renewed Protests Without Reform
detail: Former Iranian President Hassan Rouhani warned that the regime could face renewed and larger nationwide protests if it does not implement major reforms to address public grievances. The warning came as the judiciary's hardline approach to detained protesters drew criticism from reformist figures, while economic conditions continued to deteriorate with inflation above 60% and basic goods shortages spreading across multiple provinces.

title: Pentagon Briefs Congress on Iran Nuclear Threat Assessment
detail: The Pentagon delivered a classified briefing to congressional leadership on Iran's nuclear capabilities, reporting that Iran's stockpile of 440 kg of 60%-enriched uranium remained sufficient for up to ten nuclear weapons if further enriched. The briefing highlighted the ongoing fortification of the Isfahan underground complex and the IAEA's inability to verify Iranian nuclear activities since Tehran's February 2 letter rejecting inspections.
